title: Add a Short-History Sequence Specialist Over the Last 5-10 Laps
summary: Public F1 pit-stop papers are increasingly using sequence models because pit calls depend on recent pace trend, volatility, and degradation trajectory, not just the current row.
rationale: Public F1 pit-stop papers are increasingly using sequence models because pit calls depend on recent pace trend, volatility, and degradation trajectory, not just the current row. The current pipeline is mostly row-wise feature engineering, so a compact temporal specialist is a clean orthogonal addition.
implementation_hint: For each driver-race sequence, feed the last 5-10 laps of numeric features and derived deltas into a small TCN/GRU/BiLSTM specialist. Keep leakage control strict: sequence windows may use only past laps, and splits must hold out entire `Year_Race` groups.
expected_effect: Can capture pace collapse, stabilization, and pre-stop drift patterns that hand-built one-row ratios miss.
risk: If folds or padding are mishandled, sequence leakage is easy; a deep model may also overfit given only four seasons.
